master_row.title: Dimensional Ambiguity at TZP
master_row.canonical_statement: The local topological dimension at the TZP satisfies $\dim_p(M)\in\DimRange$ for an ambient $n$-dimensional manifold, and transitions between these branches are governed by the lemniscatic self-intersection.
master_row.dictionary: The local topological dimension (_p(M)) at the TZP takes values in (n,n!!1) for a surrounding (n)-dimensional manifold, and transitions between these values are governed by the lemniscatic self‑intersection.
master_row.encyclopedia: The TZP acts as a dimensional seam. It is not merely located inside a dimension; it controls the transition between neighboring effective dimensional states. Interpretive role: This is the first entry that explicitly permits extra or reduced local degrees of freedom at the zero point. It sets up the logic later used for hyper-dimensional span, measure corrections, and singular scaling laws.
